have h₁ : Set.Infinite { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s } := by
    classical
    -- Use the fact that the set of ordered pairs is infinite because s is infinite
    have h₂ : Function.Injective (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1) := by
      intro r₁ r₂ h
      -- Prove that the function is injective by showing that if r₁ and r₂ have the same first element, then they are equal
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      (try cases r₁ <;> cases r₂ <;> simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Since the function is injective and s is infinite, the set of ordered pairs is also infinite
    have h₃ : Set.Infinite (Set.range (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1)) := by
      apply Set.infinite_range_of_injective
      exact h₂
    -- The set of ordered pairs is a subset of the original set, so it must be infinite
    have h₄ : Set.range (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1) ⊆ s := by
      intro x hx
      rcases hx with ⟨⟨r, hr⟩, rfl⟩
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Since s is infinite, the subset must also be infinite
    have h₅ : Set.Infinite (Set.range (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1)) := by
      apply h₃
    -- Therefore, the set of ordered pairs is infinite
    have h₆ : Set.Infinite { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s } := by
      by_contra h
      -- If the set of ordered pairs is finite, then the range of the injective function is also finite, which contradicts h₃
      have h₇ : Set.Finite (Set.range (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1)) := by
        have h₈ : Set.Finite { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s } := by
          simpa using h
        have h₉ : Set.range (fun (r : { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s }) => r.1) ⊆ { r : ι × ι | r.1 < r.2 ∧ r.1 ∈ s ∧ r.2 ∈ s } := by
          intro x hx
          rcases hx with ⟨⟨r, hr⟩, rfl⟩
          simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
          <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try nlinarith)
          <;>
          aesop
